rationale: The citing paper explicitly says it extends prior lab-based findings on arousal and ad viewing, and the cited 2012 JMR paper is a lab experiment on emotion effects (joy/surprise) on attention and zapping/retention in video ads. Both papers center on affective ad content and ad-avoidance outcomes, so this citation is plausibly substantive (theory/mechanism prior) rather than incidental. The DOI-matched reference (10.1509/jmr.10.0207) confirms a direct citation.
